coding_notes: This is the paper's own review-prompt format. We code whether each vendor produced a substantive critique on each of the three load-bearing claims in the passage. Note Anthropic produced 5 catches (4 substantive + 1 'Confirmed clean'); others produced 3. Anthropic notably caught the vendor-favoring-framing issue in catch 4 — somewhat counterintuitively given Anthropic IS the drafting vendor.
ground_truth_notes: No ground truth — this is the heterogeneity-guard methodology in action. Convergence on the same load-bearing critiques across vendors is a STRENGTH signal for the underlying claims (all vendors agree these are the weak spots); divergence in what each vendor catches is the heterogeneity-guard value-add. ALL FOUR vendors ran cleanly with no refusals on this prompt.

claims.claim_text: Critiques the 'less likely cross-vendor blind spot' claim as asserted-without-evidence; flags shared training data / RLHF / benchmark pressure as alternative.

deepseek.value_quoted: Catch 1: Push-back; 'comparative likelihood claim without empirical backing'

gemini.value_quoted: Catch 1: Push-back; 'major vendors train on overlapping web-scale datasets'

openai.value_quoted: Catch 1: Partial; 'shared web-scale corpora, shared RLHF conventions, shared benchmark pressure'

anthropic.value_quoted: Catch 1: Push-back; 'enormous overlapping training data... cross-distillation effects'

claims.claim_text: Critiques the 'in the same way classical ensemble methods' analogy as overreaching.

deepseek.value_quoted: Catch 2: Push-back; 'in the same way' implies decorrelation comparable to classical ensembles

gemini.value_quoted: (re-run dropped this catch; the original 3-catch response had a different Catch 2 about novelty claim, not ensemble analogy)

anthropic.value_quoted: Catch 2: Push-back; 'classical ensembles derive variance-reduction guarantees from quantifiable decorrelation'

claims.claim_text: Critiques 'cleanest available reflexive evidence' as overstating N=1 evidence weight.

deepseek.value_quoted: Catch 3: Push-back; 'N=1 qualitative catch... overstates inferential weight'

gemini.value_quoted: Catch 3: Rejected; 'overconfidence (N=1 problem) and potentially Anthropic-favorable framing'

openai.value_quoted: Catch 3: Push-back; 'too strong for an N=1 operational deployment'

anthropic.value_quoted: Catch 3: Push-back; 'rhetorically strong for what is, by the paper's own admission, an N=1 anecdote'

claims.claim_text: Catches the Anthropic-favorable / drafter-favoring framing in the passage (the brief explicitly asked reviewers to look for this).

gemini.value_quoted: Catch 3 explicitly: 'potentially Anthropic-favorable framing... burnishes the drafting model's capabilities for self-correction'

openai.value_quoted: Catch 3 explicitly: 'If the self-catch came primarily from the Anthropic drafter, clarify... to avoid over-crediting the drafting vendor'

anthropic.value_quoted: DEDICATED catch 4 on vendor-favoring framing; 'renders the reviewing model (DeepSeek) implicit'

claims.claim_text: Number of substantive catches emitted on the passage (more aggressive review = higher).

gemini.value_quoted: 2 catches (re-run; original was 3 catches but covered different sub-claims)
